description_long: This object is a compact electronic module with a blue printed circuit board (PCB) base and two prominent cylindrical components on the front, each covered by a fine black mesh grille. The front face features four metal pins protruding downward, labeled for electrical connections, and a metallic rectangular component at the top center. The rear side of the board is populated with several integrated circuits, resistors, and other small electronic components, all neatly soldered onto the PCB. The cylindrical elements are symmetrically placed and dominate the front, suggesting they are transducers or sensors. The overall construction is lightweight, with most of the volume occupied by air within the cylindrical housings and the PCB.
description: A small blue electronic module with two large mesh-covered cylindrical components and a row of four metal pins for electrical connection.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large mesh-covered cylinders, four labeled metal pins, and a metallic component at the top center.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the two cylindrical components protruding from the flat PCB.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the blue PCB with multiple integrated circuits and soldered electronic components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cylindrical components' profile and the flatness of the PCB.
attribute_brand_logo: Text 'HC-SR04' is visible on the front of the PCB, but no distinct brand logo or emblem is present.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grilles have a fine, circular mesh pattern with a dense, uniform grid of small holes covering the entire face of each cylinder.
